findings.claim: The PoC tests whether a deviation feature helps a GRU, but its 'deviation adds nothing' conclusion is an unfalsifiable artifact of the synthetic construction.
findings.failure_mechanism: Fraud = 2-4 sigma spike vs card level in a 0.4-sigma-noise, length-16 sequence -> raw sequence is trivially sufficient for near-perfect detection (seq_raw 0.964). With no headroom, NO encoding can show incremental value; the null is guaranteed by the DGP, not learned. Cannot distinguish '(a) deviation adds nothing' from '(b) task too easy, any model saturates'.
findings.evidence_test: Harder variant: card level NOT recoverable from recent history (shared population mean, sigma=1.0), modest fraud spike (+0.5..1.0 sigma above unobserved true level). Then deviation is the only reliable signal; seq_raw should degrade while seq_dev holds. Plus: real data (Step 6) where signal isn't gift-wrapped.

findings.claim: The precondition 'seq beats tab (+0.539)' validates that cross-time context is the mechanism.
findings.failure_mechanism: tab_last_raw uses last-step amount ONLY, so the gap measures 'history access' not 'temporal/sequence modeling'. A tabular model with card mean/std aggregates could match the GRU, since the synthetic signal is reconstructable from (current amount, card mean). The precondition is satisfied trivially without supporting the mechanism claim.
findings.evidence_test: Add tab_aggregate baseline = logreg on [last_amount, mean(prior), std(prior)]. If it ~matches seq_raw, the GRU's edge is summary stats not temporal structure.

findings.claim: seq_raw standardization and PLE edges are computed on equal footing with the causal deviation.
findings.failure_mechanism: mu/sd and PLE edges are fit on log_amt[tr].ravel() INCLUDING the last (fraudulent) step (2-4 sigma outliers), while the deviation feature is causal (excludes current step). Asymmetric: seq_raw standardized with fraud-contaminated stats; PLE bins shaped by the fraud distribution (label implicitly influences encoding). Leakage-adjacent.
findings.evidence_test: Refit mu/sd and PLE edges on prior-history steps only (exclude labeled last step). Re-run; if seq_raw shifts >0.02 the comparison was contaminated.

findings.claim: 8-epoch fixed-lr GRU training reflects converged representations.
findings.failure_mechanism: No scheduler/early-stop/loss-curve. seq_ple (24-dim input) may be undertrained vs seq_raw (1-dim); the -0.014 PLE gap could be underfitting, not PLE being unhelpful. Comparison is of 8-epoch checkpoints, not optima.
findings.evidence_test: Plot loss curves; train to plateau; re-compare. If seq_ple shifts >0.015 the 8-epoch result is uninterpretable.

findings.claim: deviation-vs-raw parity is a general property.
findings.failure_mechanism: Running-mean estimate noise scales with sequence length; at L=16/sigma=0.4 it's tiny so both saturate. Parity may be length-dependent.
findings.evidence_test: Re-run at L=3 (noisy running mean). If seq_dev beats seq_raw at L=3 but not L=16, parity is a length artifact.

findings.failure_mechanism: No temporal/distribution shift in the PoC; PLE bins fit and tested on same distribution. Real Step-6 data has temporal amount drift. Deferred but flag.

findings.claim: mechanism figure is on held-out data.
findings.failure_mechanism: dev/y figure uses full dataset (train+test); presented next to test metrics. iid so same shape, but inconsistent.

findings.claim: PLE edge dedup may create near-zero-width bins.
findings.failure_mechanism: 1e-9 dedup could give degenerate bins in low-entropy regions; unlikely at N_BINS=24 on ~normal data.

summary: Central negative result ('deviation/PLE add nothing to a GRU') is mathematically guaranteed by the synthetic DGP (2-4 sigma spike vs 0.4 sigma noise) -> circular, uninformative either way (F1, FATAL). Tabular baseline confound (F2): seq>>tab measures history access not temporal modeling; need aggregate baseline. Standardization/PLE-edge contamination by fraud last step (F3). Convergence (F4) and length-dependence (F5) material. Resolution path: harder synthetic control + real account-sequence data + aggregate baseline + causal-only stats.
